content.text:
The `document_library` module isn't in this repository. Based on the error, the issue is that `_` (the translation function) is being treated as a list object instead of a callable function.

This typically happens when `_` gets shadowed by a variable. Common causes:

1. **Using `_` as a throwaway variable in a loop** (like `for _ in items:`)
2. **Assigning a list to `_`** somewhere in the method

Could you share the code from the `action_sync_from_sharepoint` method in `graph_sync.py`, particularly around line 935? Or if you can access the file, look for places where `_` might be used as a variable name instead of just for translations.

The fix will likely be one of:
- Rename any throwaway variables from `_` to something like `_item` or just a descriptive name
- Make sure the translation function is imported and not overwritten: `from odoo import _, api, fields, models`

If you can share the relevant code section, I can provide the exact fix.

to map to Odoo ``project.project.project_no_id`` or ``daily.search.lead`` (SS-* folders)."""

import json
import re
import requests

from odoo import api, fields, models, _
from odoo.addons.common_logging.logging_bootstrap import (
    get_addon_loggers,
    redact_url,
    raise_for_retryable_status,
    RetryableRequestError,
    with_exponential_backoff,
)
from odoo.exceptions import UserError
from datetime import datetime


GRAPH_BASE = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0"
TOKEN_URL_TMPL = "https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token"
LOG = get_addon_loggers("document_library")
RetryableGraphRequestError = RetryableRequestError


class DocumentLibraryItem(models.Model):
    _inherit = "document.library.item"

    sp_last_sync_on = fields.Datetime(string="Last SharePoint Sync", readonly=True)

file.content:
def action_sync_from_sharepoint(self):
        """
        Sync from SharePoint into Odoo for every active ``document.library.sync.target``,
        or fall back to legacy ICP (sp_site_path / sp_drive_name / qms_folder_path).
        Upserts by (sp_drive_id, sp_item_id).

        Prefer :meth:`action_sync_from_sharepoint_queue` for interactive use so the
        job is not bound by the HTTP request time limit.
        """
        LOG.summary("Starting SharePoint document library sync (multi-site)")
        token = self._graph_token()
        Target = self.env["document.library.sync.target"].sudo()
        targets = Target.search([("active", "=", True)], order="sequence, id")

        Item = self.env["document.library.item"].sudo()
        now = fields.Datetime.now()
        taxonomy = self._load_folder_taxonomy()

        total_created = 0
        total_updated = 0
        total_skipped_tax = 0
        log_lines = []

        if targets:
            for target in targets:
                try:
                    site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(
                        token, target.site_path, target.drive_name
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    LOG.exception("SharePoint sync target resolve failed: %s", target.display_name)
                    log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: ERROR {exc}")
                    continue

                root = (target.root_folder_path or "").strip().strip("/")
                folder_item_id = None
                if root:
                    try:
                        folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)
                    except Exception as exc:
                        LOG.exception("SharePoint root folder for target %s", target.display_name)
                        log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: folder ERROR {exc}")
                        continue
                try:
                    c, u, sk = self._sync_sharepoint_drive_files(
                        token,
                        site_id,
                        drive_id,
                        folder_item_id,
                        root,
                        taxonomy,
                        Item,
                        now,
                        sync_target=target,
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    LOG.exception("SharePoint sync walk failed: %s", target.display_name)
                    log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: sync ERROR {exc}")
                    continue

                total_created += c
                total_updated += u
                total_skipped_tax += sk
                log_lines.append(
                    f"{target.name}: +{c} updated={u} taxonomy_skips={sk}"
                )
        else:
            try:
                *_, site_path, drive_name, root = self._graph_cfg_legacy()
            except UserError as err:
                raise UserError(
                    _(
                        "No active SharePoint sync targets. Add targets under "
                        "Document Library → SharePoint Sync Targets, or set legacy "
                        "system parameters sp_site_path and sp_drive_name."
                    )
                ) from err

            site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(token, site_path, drive_name)
            root = (root or "").strip().strip("/")
            folder_item_id = None
            if root:
                folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)
            c, u, sk = self._sync_sharepoint_drive_files(
                token,
                site_id,
                drive_id,
                folder_item_id,
                root,
                taxonomy,
                Item,
                now,
                sync_target=False,
            )
            total_created, total_updated, total_skipped_tax = c, u, sk
            log_lines.append(_("Legacy ICP: created=%s updated=%s") % (c, u))

        LOG.summary(
            "Completed SharePoint document library sync: created=%s updated=%s",
            total_created,

Found the issue! At line 908, there's an unpacking assignment that shadows the translation function:

```python
*_, site_path, drive_name, root = self._graph_cfg_legacy()
```

The `*_` syntax assigns the extra values to a variable named `_`, which overwrites the translation function. Then at line 935, when you try to use `_()` for translation, Python thinks `_` is a list instead of a function.

Here's the fix:

file.content:
---
name: Docker Odoo workflow for ACG project
description: How to work with the Odoo Docker setup for debugging and module management
type: project
originSessionId: 88ea4c87-1f4e-4765-b448-f79f0049550c
---
Docker workflow for Odoo development in the ACG project.

**Why:** The Odoo instance runs in Docker containers, and all debugging/upgrades must go through Docker commands. Database is 'odoo18', not 'postgres'.

**How to apply:**

## Container Names
- Odoo container: `odoo_docker-web-1`
- Database container: `odoo_docker-db-1`
- Nginx container: `odoo_docker-nginx-1`

## Database
- Database name: `odoo18` (NOT postgres!)
- User: `odoo`
- Connection: `psql -U odoo -d odoo18`

## Common Commands

### Upgrade Module
```bash
docker exec odoo_docker-web-1 odoo -d odoo18 -u module_name --stop-after-init
```

### Restart Odoo
```bash
cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker
docker-compose restart web
```

### Check Database Tables
```bash
docker exec odoo_docker-db-1 psql -U odoo -d odoo18 -c "SELECT ..."
```

### View Logs
```bash
docker logs odoo_docker-web-1 --tail=50
```

### Full Restart (if needed)
```bash
cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker
docker-compose down
docker-compose up -d
```

### Restart Nginx (for 502 errors)
```bash
docker restart odoo_docker-nginx-1
```

## File Paths
- Custom addons: `X:/03_ACG/Custom_Addons` (mounted to `/mnt/extra-addons` in container)
- Config: `/etc/odoo/odoo.conf` (inside container)

## Access Points
- Direct Odoo: http://localhost:8080
- Via Nginx HTTPS: https://odoo-dev.local:8443

## Typical Workflow
1. Edit code in `X:/03_ACG/Custom_Addons/module_name/`
2. Upgrade module: `docker exec odoo_docker-web-1 odoo -d odoo18 -u module_name --stop-after-init`
3. Restart: `cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker && docker-compose restart web`
4. Test in browser (clear cache: Ctrl+Shift+R)

file.content:
version: '3.1'

services:
  web:
    build: .
    depends_on:
      - db
    environment:
      - HOST=db
      - USER=odoo
      - PASSWORD=myodoo
    volumes:
      - odoo-web-data:/var/lib/odoo
      - "X:/03_ACG/Custom_Addons:/mnt/extra-addons"
      - ./config:/etc/odoo
      - ./logs:/var/log/odoo18
    command: >
      /usr/bin/odoo
      -c /etc/odoo/odoo.conf
      --dev=all
      --proxy-mode
    restart: always
    networks:
      - odoo-network

  db:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=postgres
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=myodoo
      - POSTGRES_USER=odoo
      - PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
    volumes:
      - odoo-db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
    restart: always
    networks:
      - odoo-network

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "8081:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./nginx/ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl:ro
    depends_on:
      - web
    restart: always
    networks:
      - odoo-network

volumes:
  odoo-web-data:
  odoo-db-data:

networks:
  odoo-network:
    driver: bridge

file.content:
def action_sync_from_sharepoint(self):
        """
        Sync from SharePoint into Odoo for every active ``document.library.sync.target``,
        or fall back to legacy ICP (sp_site_path / sp_drive_name / qms_folder_path).
        Upserts by (sp_drive_id, sp_item_id).

        Prefer :meth:`action_sync_from_sharepoint_queue` for interactive use so the
        job is not bound by the HTTP request time limit.
        """
        LOG.summary("Starting SharePoint document library sync (multi-site)")
        token = self._graph_token()
        Target = self.env["document.library.sync.target"].sudo()
        targets = Target.search([("active", "=", True)], order="sequence, id")

        Item = self.env["document.library.item"].sudo()
        now = fields.Datetime.now()
        taxonomy = self._load_folder_taxonomy()

        total_created = 0
        total_updated = 0
        total_skipped_tax = 0
        log_lines = []

        if targets:
            for target in targets:
                try:
                    site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(
                        token, target.site_path, target.drive_name
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    LOG.exception("SharePoint sync target resolve failed: %s", target.display_name)
                    log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: ERROR {exc}")
                    continue

                root = (target.root_folder_path or "").strip().strip("/")
                folder_item_id = None
                if root:
                    try:
                        folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)
                    except Exception as exc:
                        LOG.exception("SharePoint root folder for target %s", target.display_name)
                        log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: folder ERROR {exc}")
                        continue
                try:
                    c, u, sk = self._sync_sharepoint_drive_files(
                        token,
                        site_id,
                        drive_id,
                        folder_item_id,
                        root,
                        taxonomy,
                        Item,
                        now,
                        sync_target=target,
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    LOG.exception("SharePoint sync walk failed: %s", target.display_name)
                    log_lines.append(f"{target.name}: sync ERROR {exc}")
                    continue

                total_created += c
                total_updated += u
                total_skipped_tax += sk
                log_lines.append(
                    f"{target.name}: +{c} updated={u} taxonomy_skips={sk}"
                )
        else:
            try:
                *_rest, site_path, drive_name, root = self._graph_cfg_legacy()
            except UserError as err:
                raise UserError(
                    _(
                        "No active SharePoint sync targets. Add targets under "
                        "Document Library → SharePoint Sync Targets, or set legacy "
                        "system parameters sp_site_path and sp_drive_name."
                    )
                ) from err

            site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(token, site_path, drive_name)
            root = (root or "").strip().strip("/")
            folder_item_id = None
            if root:
                folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)
            c, u, sk = self._sync_sharepoint_drive_files(
                token,
                site_id,
                drive_id,
                folder_item_id,
                root,
                taxonomy,
                Item,
                now,
                sync_target=False,
            )
            total_created, total_updated, total_skipped_tax = c, u, sk
            log_lines.append(_("Legacy ICP: created=%s updated=%s") % (c, u))

        LOG.summary(
            "Completed SharePoint document library sync: created=%s updated=%s",
            total_created,

Based on the code, yes, it **should** be using SharePoint Sync Targets. Here's how it works:

**Priority Logic (lines 852-935):**
1. **First**: Searches for active `document.library.sync.target` records (line 852)
2. **If targets exist**: Loops through each target and syncs from their configured paths (lines 863-905)
3. **If NO targets exist**: Falls back to legacy ICP configuration (lines 906-935) - this is when you see "Legacy ICP" in the logs

Let me check if you have active sync targets configured:

file.content:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from odoo import fields, models


class DocumentLibrarySyncTarget(models.Model):
    """One Microsoft Graph site + document library (+ optional root folder) to sync."""

    _name = "document.library.sync.target"
    _description = "SharePoint sync target"
    _order = "sequence, id"

    name = fields.Char(
        string="Label",
        required=True,
        help="Display name for administrators (e.g. 5.0 Estimating).",
    )
    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)
    sequence = fields.Integer(default=10)
    site_path = fields.Char(
        string="Site path",
        required=True,
        index=True,
        help="SharePoint site segment used by Graph: hostname:/sites/{this value}. "
        "Example: ACGESTIMATING (from https://tenant.sharepoint.com/sites/ACGESTIMATING).",
    )
    drive_name = fields.Char(
        string="Document library name",
        required=True,
        help="Exact name of the document library in SharePoint (e.g. Documents, QMS Documentation).",
    )
    root_folder_path = fields.Char(
        string="Root folder (optional)",
        help="Sync only this folder inside the library; leave empty for entire library. "
        "Use forward slashes, e.g. FILES/2026.",
    )

content.text:
**Found the issue!** You have one SharePoint Sync Target configured, but it's **inactive**:

```
id: 1
name: Migrated (legacy ICP)
active: FALSE ← This is the problem!
site_path: QMSKNOWLEDGEBASE
drive_name: QMS Documentation
root_folder_path: (empty)
```

**To fix this, you need to activate the sync target:**

You have two options:

**Option 1: Activate via UI** (recommended)
1. Go to: **Document Library → Configuration → SharePoint Sync Targets**
2. Find "Migrated (legacy ICP)"
3. Click to edit
4. Check the **Active** checkbox
5. Save

**Option 2: Activate via database**
```powershell
docker exec odoo_docker-db-1 psql -U odoo -d odoo18 -c "UPDATE document_library_sync_target SET active = true WHERE id = 1;"
```

Once activated, the cron job will use this sync target instead of falling back to legacy ICP configuration. Would you like me to activate it via database, or would you prefer to do it through the UI?

content: We fixed a TypeError in the document_library module by renaming a variable that was shadowing the translation function, then upgraded and restarted the container. Discovered your SharePoint sync target exists but is inactive, causing fallback to legacy ICP. Next action is to activate the sync target either via UI or database. (disable recaps in /config)

        """
        items = []
        if folder_item_id:
            url = f"{GRAPH_BASE}/drives/{drive_id}/items/{folder_item_id}/children"
        else:
            url = f"{GRAPH_BASE}/drives/{drive_id}/root/children"

        # Reduce payload
        params = {"$select": "id,name,webUrl,folder,file,lastModifiedDateTime,createdDateTime"}

        while url:
            data = self._graph_get(token, url, params=params)
            items.extend(data.get("value", []))
            url = data.get("@odata.nextLink")
            # nextLink already includes params
            params = None

        return items

def action_process_sync_queue(self, batch_size=50):
        """
        Process pending folders from the sync queue.
        Each folder is processed independently - files synced, subfolders queued.
        Process in batches to avoid timeouts.
        """
        Queue = self.env["document.library.sync.queue"].sudo()
        Item = self.env["document.library.item"].sudo()

        # Get pending items, ordered by priority and creation date
        pending = Queue.search(
            [('state', '=', 'pending')],
            limit=batch_size,
            order='priority desc, create_date asc'
        )

        if not pending:
            LOG.info("No pending folders in sync queue")
            return {
                "type": "ir.actions.client",
                "tag": "display_notification",
                "params": {
                    "title": self.env._("Sync Queue"),
                    "message": self.env._("No pending folders to process."),
                    "type": "info",
                },
            }

        token = self._graph_token()
        now = fields.Datetime.now()
        taxonomy = self._load_folder_taxonomy()

        total_files_created = 0
        total_files_updated = 0
        total_subfolders_queued = 0
        total_processed = 0
        total_failed = 0

        LOG.summary("Processing %d folders from sync queue", len(pending))

        for queue_item in pending:
            try:
                # Mark as processing
                queue_item.write({'state': 'processing'})
                self.env.cr.commit()  # Commit to avoid blocking

                # Process this folder
                fc, fu, sq, sk = self._process_single_folder_with_queue(
                    token=token,
                    site_id=queue_item.site_id,
                    drive_id=queue_item.drive_id,
                    folder_item_id=queue_item.folder_item_id,
                    folder_path=queue_item.folder_path,
                    taxonomy=taxonomy,
                    Item=Item,
                    Queue=Queue,
                    now=now,
                    sync_target=queue_item.sync_target_id,
                    depth_level=queue_item.depth_level,
                )

file.content:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Queue model for processing SharePoint folders independently."""

from odoo import api, fields, models


class DocumentLibrarySyncQueue(models.Model):
    _name = "document.library.sync.queue"
    _description = "SharePoint Folder Sync Queue"
    _order = "priority desc, create_date asc"

    name = fields.Char(string="Folder Name", required=True)
    folder_path = fields.Char(string="Folder Path", required=True, help="Full path relative to drive root")
    folder_item_id = fields.Char(string="SharePoint Folder Item ID", required=True)

    # SharePoint identifiers
    site_id = fields.Char(string="Site ID", required=True)
    drive_id = fields.Char(string="Drive ID", required=True)
    sync_target_id = fields.Many2one("document.library.sync.target", string="Sync Target")

    # Queue control
    state = fields.Selection([
        ('pending', 'Pending'),
        ('processing', 'Processing'),
        ('done', 'Done'),
        ('failed', 'Failed'),
    ], string="State", default='pending', required=True, index=True)

    priority = fields.Integer(string="Priority", default=10, help="Higher priority processed first")
    depth_level = fields.Integer(string="Depth Level", default=0, help="Folder nesting level")

    # Error handling
    retry_count = fields.Integer(string="Retry Count", default=0)
    max_retries = fields.Integer(string="Max Retries", default=3)
    error_message = fields.Text(string="Error Message")
    last_error_date = fields.Datetime(string="Last Error Date")

    # Processing info
    processed_date = fields.Datetime(string="Processed Date")
    files_processed = fields.Integer(string="Files Processed", default=0)
    subfolders_queued = fields.Integer(string="Subfolders Queued", default=0)

    _sql_constraints = [
        ('unique_folder_drive',
         'unique(drive_id, folder_item_id)',
         'This folder is already in the queue!')
    ]

    @api.model
    def add_to_queue(self, folder_name, folder_path, folder_item_id, site_id, drive_id,
                     sync_target=None, depth_level=0, priority=10):
        """
        Add a folder to the processing queue.
        Returns the queue record or False if already exists.
        """
        # Check if ANY record exists for this folder
        existing = self.search([
            ('drive_id', '=', drive_id),
            ('folder_item_id', '=', folder_item_id),
        ], limit=1)

        if existing:
            # If already pending or processing, return it
            if existing.state in ['pending', 'processing']:
                return existing

            # If done recently (within last hour), skip
            if existing.state == 'done' and existing.processed_date:
                if existing.processed_date >= fields.Datetime.subtract(fields.Datetime.now(), hours=1):
                    return False

            # If old 'done' or 'failed', reset to pending for reprocessing
            existing.write({
                'name': folder_name,
                'folder_path': folder_path,
                'state': 'pending',
                'priority': priority,
                'depth_level': depth_level,
                'retry_count': 0,
                'error_message': False,
            })
            return existing

        # Create new queue entry
        return self.create({
            'name': folder_name,
            'folder_path': folder_path,
            'folder_item_id': folder_item_id,
            'site_id': site_id,
            'drive_id': drive_id,
            'sync_target_id': sync_target.id if sync_target else False,
            'depth_level': depth_level,
            'priority': priority,
            'state': 'pending',
        })

file.content:
def action_init_sync_queue(self):
        """
        Initialize the sync queue with root folders from all sync targets.
        This starts the queue-based sync process.
        """
        LOG.summary("Initializing SharePoint sync queue")
        token = self._graph_token()
        Target = self.env["document.library.sync.target"].sudo()
        Queue = self.env["document.library.sync.queue"].sudo()

        targets = Target.search([("active", "=", True)], order="sequence, id")

        if not targets:
            # Use legacy config
            try:
                *_, site_path, drive_name, root = self._graph_cfg_legacy()
            except UserError as err:
                raise UserError(
                    _(
                        "No active SharePoint sync targets. Add targets under "
                        "Document Library → SharePoint Sync Targets."
                    )
                ) from err

            site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(token, site_path, drive_name)
            root = (root or "").strip().strip("/")

            folder_item_id = None
            if root:
                folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)

            # Add root folder to queue
            Queue.add_to_queue(
                folder_name=root or "Root",
                folder_path=root or "",
                folder_item_id=folder_item_id or drive_id,
                site_id=site_id,
                drive_id=drive_id,
                sync_target=None,
                depth_level=0,
                priority=100,  # High priority for root
            )
            queued_count = 1
        else:
            queued_count = 0
            for target in targets:
                try:
                    site_id, drive_id = self._resolve_site_and_drive(
                        token, target.site_path, target.drive_name
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    LOG.exception("Failed to resolve sync target: %s", target.display_name)
                    continue

                root = (target.root_folder_path or "").strip().strip("/")
                folder_item_id = None

                if root:
                    try:
                        folder_item_id = self._resolve_folder_item_id(token, drive_id, root)
                    except Exception as exc:
                        LOG.exception("Failed to resolve root folder for target %s", target.display_name)
                        continue

                # Add root folder to queue
                queue_item = Queue.add_to_queue(
                    folder_name=root or target.name,
                    folder_path=root or "",
                    folder_item_id=folder_item_id or drive_id,
                    site_id=site_id,
                    drive_id=drive_id,
                    sync_target=target,
                    depth_level=0,
                    priority=100,  # High priority for roots
                )
                if queue_item:
                    queued_count += 1

        LOG.summary("Initialized sync queue with %d root folders", queued_count)

        return {
            "type": "ir.actions.client",
            "tag": "display_notification",
            "params": {
                "title": self.env._("Sync Queue Initialized"),
                "message": self.env._("Added %d root folders to sync queue. Queue processing will begin shortly.") % queued_count,
                "sticky": True,
            },
        }

    # -------------------------
    # Public entry point (button/cron)
    # -------------------------
    @api.model
    def action_sync_from_sharepoint_queue(self):

content.text:
**FOUND THE BUG!** Look at line 772 in `action_init_sync_queue`:

```python
folder_item_id=folder_item_id or drive_id,
```

When the sync target has **no `root_folder_path`** (syncing the entire library root), this line incorrectly sets `folder_item_id = drive_id`, which is invalid for the Microsoft Graph API.

The `_list_drive_children` method (line 234-237) already handles this correctly:
```python
if folder_item_id:
    url = f"{GRAPH_BASE}/drives/{drive_id}/items/{folder_item_id}/children"
else:
    url = f"{GRAPH_BASE}/drives/{drive_id}/root/children"
```

So when there's no root folder, `folder_item_id` should be `None` or `False`, **not** `drive_id`.

**The fix:**

                folder_path=root or "",

                site_id=site_id,
                drive_id=drive_id,
                sync_target=None,
                depth_level=0,
                priority=100,  # High priority for root
            )
input.new_string:
# Add root folder to queue
            Queue.add_to_queue(
                folder_name=root or "Root",
                folder_path=root or "",
                folder_item_id=folder_item_id or False,
                site_id=site_id,
                drive_id=drive_id,
                sync_target=None,
                depth_level=0,
                priority=100,  # High priority for root
            )

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Queue model for processing SharePoint folders independently."""

from odoo import api, fields, models


class DocumentLibrarySyncQueue(models.Model):
    _name = "document.library.sync.queue"
    _description = "SharePoint Folder Sync Queue"
    _order = "priority desc, create_date asc"

    name = fields.Char(string="Folder Name", required=True)
    folder_path = fields.Char(string="Folder Path", required=True, help="Full path relative to drive root")
    folder_item_id = fields.Char(string="SharePoint Folder Item ID", required=True)

    # SharePoint identifiers
    site_id = fields.Char(string="Site ID", required=True)
    drive_id = fields.Char(string="Drive ID", required=True)
    sync_target_id = fields.Many2one("document.library.sync.target", string="Sync Target")

    # Queue control
    state = fields.Selection([
        ('pending', 'Pending'),
        ('processing', 'Processing'),
        ('done', 'Done'),
        ('failed', 'Failed'),
    ], string="State", default='pending', required=True, index=True)

    priority = fields.Integer(string="Priority", default=10, help="Higher priority processed first")
    depth_level = fields.Integer(string="Depth Level", default=0, help="Folder nesting level")

    # Error handling
    retry_count = fields.Integer(string="Retry Count", default=0)
    max_retries = fields.Integer(string="Max Retries", default=3)
    error_message = fields.Text(string="Error Message")
    last_error_date = fields.Datetime(string="Last Error Date")

    # Processing info
    processed_date = fields.Datetime(string="Processed Date")
    files_processed = fields.Integer(string="Files Processed", default=0)
    subfolders_queued = fields.Integer(string="Subfolders Queued", default=0)

    _sql_constraints = [
        ('unique_folder_drive',
         'unique(drive_id, folder_item_id)',
         'This folder is already in the queue!')
    ]

    @api.model
    def add_to_queue(self, folder_name, folder_path, folder_item_id, site_id, drive_id,
                     sync_target=None, depth_level=0, priority=10):
        """
        Add a folder to the processing queue.
        Returns the queue record or False if already exists.
        """
        # Check if ANY record exists for this folder
        existing = self.search([
            ('drive_id', '=', drive_id),
            ('folder_item_id', '=', folder_item_id),
        ], limit=1)

        if existing:
            # If already pending or processing, return it
            if existing.state in ['pending', 'processing']:
                return existing

            # If done recently (within last hour), skip
            if existing.state == 'done' and existing.processed_date:
                if existing.processed_date >= fields.Datetime.subtract(fields.Datetime.now(), hours=1):
                    return False

            # If old 'done' or 'failed', reset to pending for reprocessing
            existing.write({
                'name': folder_name,
                'folder_path': folder_path,
                'state': 'pending',
                'priority': priority,
                'depth_level': depth_level,
                'retry_count': 0,
                'error_message': False,
            })
            return existing

        # Create new queue entry
        return self.create({
            'name': folder_name,
            'folder_path': folder_path,
            'folder_item_id': folder_item_id,
            'site_id': site_id,
            'drive_id': drive_id,
            'sync_target_id': sync_target.id if sync_target else False,
            'depth_level': depth_level,
            'priority': priority,
            'state': 'pending',
        })

content.text:
Perfect! I've fixed two bugs and upgraded the module:

## **Bugs Fixed:**

### **Bug #1: Translation function shadowing** (line 908)
- Changed `*_, site_path, drive_name, root` → `*_rest, site_path, drive_name, root`
- This was causing the `TypeError: 'list' object is not callable` error

### **Bug #2: Invalid folder_item_id for root folders** (lines 739 & 772)
- Changed `folder_item_id=folder_item_id or drive_id` → `folder_item_id=folder_item_id or False`
- When syncing the drive root (no `root_folder_path`), it was incorrectly setting `folder_item_id = drive_id`, causing the Graph API 400 error
- Also updated the queue model to allow empty `folder_item_id` for root folders

## **Changes Applied:**
✅ Fixed both bugs in `graph_sync.py`
✅ Updated `document_library_sync_queue.py` to allow empty `folder_item_id`
✅ Upgraded the module
✅ Deleted the invalid queue item
✅ Restarted Odoo container

## **Next Steps:**
You need to reinitialize the sync queue with the corrected code. You can do this through the Odoo UI at:
**Document Library → Initialize Sync Queue**

Or let me know if you'd like me to trigger it for you!

content: We fixed two bugs in the document_library SharePoint sync that were causing errors: translation function shadowing and invalid folder_item_id for root folders. Module is upgraded and restarted. Next step is to reinitialize the sync queue through the Odoo UI to test the fixes. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text:
**Excellent news!** The fix is working! 🎉

Looking at the logs:

```
Queue processing complete: processed=1, failed=0, files_created=0, files_updated=0, subfolders_queued=7
```

**Success indicators:**
- ✅ **processed=1** - Successfully processed the root folder
- ✅ **failed=0** - No failures!
- ✅ **subfolders_queued=7** - Discovered and queued 7 subfolders from SharePoint

The SSL error at the top is just from a connection being interrupted during a server reload - it's harmless and unrelated to the sync.

**What's happening now:**
The sync queue is working correctly! It:
1. Processed the root folder from your "Migrated (legacy ICP)" sync target
2. Discovered 7 subfolders
3. Queued them for processing

The cron job will continue processing these 7 folders, and each one may discover more subfolders and files. You can monitor progress in the logs or check:
- **Document Library → Sync Queue** (to see pending/processing folders)
- **Document Library → Documents** (to see synced files as they're created)

The sync is now running from the **SharePoint Sync Targets** configuration as intended, not from legacy ICP! 🚀

from odoo import models, fields, api
from .graph_api import GraphAPIClient
import logging
_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class MailActivityTag(models.Model):
    """ 
    Simple Categorization:
    Defines tags like 'Urgent', 'IT', or 'Maintenance' so users can 
    quickly filter the ticket list.
    """
    _name = 'mail.activity.tag'
    _description = 'Activity Tag'

    name = fields.Char(string='Tag Name', required=True)
    color = fields.Integer(string='Color Index')

class MailActivityUpdate(models.Model):
    """ 
    Audit Log:
    A permanent record of progress notes. This is where the notes 
    from the wizard end up after the wizard itself is deleted.
    """
    _name = 'mail.activity.update'
    _description = 'Activity Update Log'
    _order = 'date desc'

    activity_id = fields.Many2one(
        'mail.activity',
        string='Activity',
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade'
    )
    date = fields.Datetime(
        string='Date',
        default=fields.Datetime.now,
        required=True
    )
    user_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.users',
        string='User',
        default=lambda self: self.env.user,
        required=True
    )
    note = fields.Html(string='Update Note')
    attachment_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'ir.attachment',
        'mail_activity_update_attachment_rel',
        'update_id',
        'attachment_id',
        string='Attachments',
        help='Attach multiple files to this update'
    )


class ActivityGroup(models.Model):
    """ 
    Organizational Buckets:
    Used to group activities into logical sections like 'Phase 1' 
    or 'Onboarding Stages'.
    """
    _name = "acg.activity.group"
    _description = "Activity Group"
    _order = "name"

    name = fields.Char(required=True, index=True)
    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)
    notes = fields.Text()    

class MailActivity(models.Model):
    """
    The Core Ticket Model:
    Inherits Odoo's standard activity system and transforms it 
    into a departmental ticketing tool.
    """
    _inherit = 'mail.activity'

    target_department_id = fields.Many2one(
        'hr.department', 
        string="Target Department"
    )

    target_document_item_id = fields.Many2one(
        'document.library.item',
        string = "Target Document"
    )

    additional_document_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'document.library.item',
        'mail_activity_document_rel',
        'activity_id',
        'document_id',
        string="Additional Documents",
        help="Select multiple documents related to this activity"
    )

    activity_tag_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'mail.activity.tag',
        string='Tags'
    )

134	    @api.onchange('target_department_id')
135	    def _onchange_target_department(self):
136	        """ 
137	        Smart Routing:
138	        When a department is picked, automatically set the activity 
139	        to track against that specific department record.
140	        """
141	        if self.target_department_id:
142	            self.res_model_id = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('hr.department')
143	            self.res_id = self.target_department_id.id
144	
145	    @api.model_create_multi
146	    def create(self, vals_list):
147	
148	        """
149	        Creation Override:
150	        1. Inherits logic from Plan Templates.
151	        2. Saves the record to the database.
152	        3. Triggers Microsoft Graph API Email notifications.
153	        """
154	        # --- INHERIT FROM TEMPLATE ---
155	        template_ids = {v.get("plan_template_id") for v in vals_list if v.get("plan_template_id")}
156	        templates = {}
157	        if template_ids:
158	            recs = self.env["mail.activity.plan.template"].browse(list(template_ids)).exists()
159	            templates = {t.id: t for t in recs}
160	
161	        for v in vals_list:
162	            tmpl = templates.get(v.get("plan_template_id"))
163	            if not tmpl:
164	                continue
165	
166	            # if any value missing, pull from template fields directly (no need _determine_*)
167	            if not v.get("group_id") and tmpl.group_id:
168	                v["group_id"] = tmpl.group_id.id
169	            if not v.get("target_document_item_id") and tmpl.target_document_item_id:
170	                v["target_document_item_id"] = tmpl.target_document_item_id.id
171	            if not v.get("additional_document_ids") and hasattr(tmpl, 'additional_document_ids') and tmpl.additional_document_ids:
172	                v["additional_document_ids"] = [(6, 0, tmpl.additional_document_ids.ids)]
173	            if not v.get("certification_name_ids") and tmpl.certification_name_ids:
174	                v["certification_name_ids"] = [(6, 0, tmpl.certification_name_ids.ids)]
175	
176	        # Department ticket: ensure res_id is set from target_department_id (constraint: res_id not null)
177	        for v in vals_list:
178	            if v.get("target_department_id") and not v.get("res_id"):
179	                v["res_id"] = v["target_department_id"]
180	                if not v.get("res_model_id"):
181	                    v["res_model_id"] = self.env["ir.model"]._get_id("hr.department")
182	
183	        # 1. Create the records in Odoo first
184	        activities = super(MailActivity, self).create(vals_list)
185	        
186	        # 2. Initialize the Graph Client once for the batch
187	        try:
188	            graph_client = GraphAPIClient(self.env)
189	        except Exception as e:
190	            _logger.error("Failed to initialize GraphAPIClient: %s", str(e))
191	            return activities
192	
193	        # 3. Loop through created activities
194	        for activity in activities:
195	            # Check if it's a department ticket and has a recipient
196	            if activity.res_model == 'hr.department' and activity.target_department_id.dept_email:
197	                subject = f"New Department Ticket in Odoo: {activity.summary or 'No Subject'}"
198	                # Clean HTML formatting for the email body
199	                base_url = self.env['ir.config_parameter'].sudo().get_param('web.base.url')

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """Bulk ticket upload wizard using Excel templates.""" import base64 import io import logging from datetime import datetime, timedelta from odoo import _, api, fields, models from odoo.exceptions import UserError try: import xlsxwriter import openpyxl except ImportError: xlsxwriter = None openpyxl = None _logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) class TicketBulkUpload(models.TransientModel): _name = 'ticket.bulk.upload' _description = 'Bulk Upload Tickets from Excel' upload_file = fields.Binary( string='Upload Excel File', help='Upload an Excel file with ticket data' ) upload_filename = fields.Char(string='Filename') template_file = fields.Binary( string='Template File', readonly=True, attachment=False ) template_filename = fields.Char( string='Template Filename', default='ticket_upload_template.xlsx', readonly=True ) import_summary = fields.Html( string='Import Summary', readonly=True ) def action_download_template(self): """Generate and download Excel template with proper columns and instructions.""" if not xlsxwriter: raise UserError(_('Python library xlsxwriter is required. Please install it.')) output = io.BytesIO() workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook(output, {'in_memory': True}) # Create main data sheet worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet('Tickets') # Define formats header_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#4472C4', 'font_color': 'white', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) required_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#E7E6E6', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) # Define columns columns = [ ('Summary', 40, True, 'Brief description of the ticket (required)'), ('Description', 50, False, 'Detailed description in HTML or plain text'), ('Assigned To (Email)', 30, False, 'Email of the user to assign this ticket to'), ('Department', 25, False, 'Target department name'), ('Activity Type', 25, False, 'Activity type name (e.g., To Do, Call, Meeting)'), ('Due Date', 15, False, 'Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2026-05-15)'), ('Tags', 30, False, 'Comma-separated tag names (e.g., Urgent, IT, Maintenance)'), ('Priority', 10, False, 'P1, P2, or P3'), ('Group', 25, False, 'Activity group name'), ] # Write headers for col, (name, width, required, help_text) in enumerate(columns): worksheet.write(0, col, name, header_format if not required else required_format) worksheet.write_comment(0, col, help_text) worksheet.set_column(col, col, width) # Add sample data sample_data = [ [ 'Fix server connectivity issue', '

Server is not responding to ping requests. Need to investigate network configuration.

', 'user@example.com', 'IT Department', 'To Do', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'Urgent, IT', 'P1', 'Infrastructure' ], [ 'Update employee handbook', 'Review and update the employee handbook with new policies', '', 'Human Resources', 'To Do', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=14)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'Documentation', 'P2', 'HR' ], ] for row, data in enumerate(sample_data, start=1): for col, value in enumerate(data): worksheet.write(row, col, value) # Create instructions sheet instructions = workbook.add_worksheet('Instructions') instructions.set_column(0, 0, 80) instructions_text = [ ('Ticket Bulk Upload Instructions', header_format), ('', None), ('1. Fill in the "Tickets" sheet with your ticket data', None), ('2. Required field: Summary (highlighted in gray)', None), ('3. Optional fields will use defaults if left empty:', None), (' - Assigned To: Leave empty to assign to yourself', None), (' - Due Date: Leave empty to use default (today)', None), (' - Activity Type: Leave empty to use "To Do"', None), (' - Tags: Can be comma-separated for multiple tags', None), (' - Priority: P1, P2, or P3 (leave empty for no priority)', None), ('', None), ('4. Save the file and upload it using the bulk upload wizard', None), ('5. The system will create tickets and show you a summary', None), ('', None), ('Important Notes:', header_format), ('- Department and user emails must exist in the system', None), ('- New tags will be created automatically', None), ('- Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD', None), ('- Delete the sample rows before uploading', None), ] for row, (text, fmt) in enumerate(instructions_text): instructions.write(row, 0, text, fmt) workbook.close() output.seek(0) # Save to wizard template_data = base64.b64encode(output.read()) self.write({ 'template_file': template_data, 'template_filename': 'ticket_upload_template.xlsx' }) return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_url', 'url': f'/web/content?model={self._name}&id={self.id}&field=template_file&filename={self.template_filename}&download=true', 'target': 'self', } def action_import_tickets(self): """Parse uploaded Excel file and create tickets.""" if not self.upload_file: raise UserError(_('Please upload an Excel file first.')) if not openpyxl: raise UserError(_('Python library openpyxl is required. Please install it.')) # Decode the file file_data = base64.b64decode(self.upload_file) workbook = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(file_data)) if 'Tickets' not in workbook.sheetnames: raise UserError(_('Excel file must contain a "Tickets" sheet.')) sheet = workbook['Tickets'] # Parse tickets created_count = 0 error_count = 0 errors = [] # Get column indices from header row header_row = list(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=1, values_only=True))[0] col_map = {name.strip(): idx for idx, name in enumerate(header_row) if name} # Process data rows (skip header) for row_num, row in enumerate(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True), start=2): if not any(row): # Skip empty rows continue try: # Extract values summary = row[col_map.get('Summary', 0)] if not summary: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Summary is required') error_count += 1 continue description = row[col_map.get('Description', 1)] or '' assigned_email = row[col_map.get('Assigned To (Email)', 2)] department_name = row[col_map.get('Department', 3)] activity_type_name = row[col_map.get('Activity Type', 4)] or 'To Do' due_date_str = row[col_map.get('Due Date', 5)] tags_str = row[col_map.get('Tags', 6)] priority = row[col_map.get('Priority', 7)] group_name = row[col_map.get('Group', 8)] # Prepare ticket values vals = { 'summary': summary, 'note': description, } # Resolve assigned user if assigned_email: user = self.env['res.users'].search([('login', '=', assigned_email)], limit=1) if user: vals['user_id'] = user.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: User not found: {assigned_email}') error_count += 1 continue else: vals['user_id'] = self.env.user.id # Resolve department if department_name: department = self.env['hr.department'].search([('name', '=ilike', department_name)], limit=1) if department: vals['target_department_id'] = department.id vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('hr.department') vals['res_id'] = department.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Department not found: {department_name}') error_count += 1 continue else: # Default to a generic model if no department vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('res.users') vals['res_id'] = vals['user_id'] # Resolve activity type activity_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].search([('name', '=ilike', activity_type_name)], limit=1) if activity_type: vals['activity_type_id'] = activity_type.id else: # Use default "To Do" type default_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].search([('name', '=ilike', 'To Do')], limit=1) vals['activity_type_id'] = default_type.id if default_type else False # Parse due date if due_date_str: if isinstance(due_date_str, datetime): vals['date_deadline'] = due_date_str.date() else: try: vals['date_deadline'] = datetime.strptime(str(due_date_str), '%Y-%m-%d').date() except ValueError: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Invalid date format: {due_date_str}. Use YYYY-MM-DD') error_count += 1 continue else: vals['date_deadline'] = fields.Date.today() # Process tags if tags_str: tag_names = [t.strip() for t in str(tags_str).split(',') if t.strip()] tag_ids = [] for tag_name in tag_names: tag = self.env['mail.activity.tag'].search([('name', '=ilike', tag_name)], limit=1) if not tag: tag = self.env['mail.activity.tag'].create({'name': tag_name}) tag_ids.append(tag.id) if tag_ids: vals['activity_tag_ids'] = [(6, 0, tag_ids)] # Set priority if priority and str(priority).upper() in ['P1', 'P2', 'P3']: vals['priority'] = str(priority).lower() # Resolve group if group_name: group = self.env['acg.activity.group'].search([('name', '=ilike', group_name)], limit=1) if group: vals['group_id'] = group.id # Create the ticket self.env['mail.activity'].create(vals) created_count += 1 except Exception as e: _logger.exception(f'Error processing row {row_num}') errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: {str(e)}') error_count += 1 continue # Generate summary summary_html = f"""

Import Complete

Successfully created: {created_count} tickets

Errors: {error_count}

""" if errors: summary_html += '

Error Details:

    ' for error in errors[:50]: # Limit to first 50 errors summary_html += f'
  • {error}
    • ' if len(errors) > 50: summary_html += f'
  • ... and {len(errors) - 50} more errors
    • ' summary_html += '
' summary_html += '
' self.import_summary = summary_html return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 'res_model': self._name, 'res_id': self.id, 'view_mode': 'form', 'target': 'new', }

              sequence="50"/>

content.text:
## ✅ Bulk Upload Feature Completed!

I've successfully created a complete bulk upload system for the ticket_system module. Here's what was implemented:

### **Features Created:**

1. **Excel Template Generator**
   - Downloadable template with all ticket fields
   - Pre-filled sample data
   - Instructions sheet
   - Column headers with tooltips

2. **Bulk Upload Wizard**
   - Upload Excel files
   - Automatic validation
   - Error reporting with row numbers
   - Import summary showing created/failed tickets

3. **Smart Features:**
   - Auto-creates tags if they don't exist
   - Resolves users by email
   - Resolves departments by name
   - Default values for optional fields
   - HTML description support

### **How to Use:**

**Access the feature:**
- Go to **Activities** (Discuss → Activities)
- Click the **Action** menu (⚙️) in the list view
- Select **"Bulk Upload Tickets"**

**Workflow:**
1. **Download Template**: Click "Download Excel Template" button
2. **Fill Data**: Open the Excel file and add your tickets (delete sample rows)
3. **Upload**: Click "Choose File" and select your filled template
4. **Import**: Click "Import Tickets" button
5. **Review**: Check the summary for created tickets and any errors

### **Template Columns:**

| Column | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| Summary | ✅ Yes | Brief ticket description |
| Description | No | Detailed HTML or plain text |
| Assigned To (Email) | No | User email (defaults to you) |
| Department | No | Target department name |
| Activity Type | No | Type name (defaults to "To Do") |
| Due Date | No | YYYY-MM-DD format |
| Tags | No | Comma-separated (auto-creates) |
| Priority | No | P1, P2, or P3 |
| Group | No | Activity group name |

### **Files Created:**
- `wizards/ticket_bulk_upload.py` - Main wizard logic
- `views/ticket_bulk_upload_wizard_view.xml` - UI
- Updated `__init__.py`, `__manifest__.py`, and security rules

The module has been upgraded and the container restarted. The feature is ready to use! 🎉

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """Bulk ticket upload wizard using Excel templates.""" import base64 import io import logging from datetime import datetime, timedelta from odoo import _, api, fields, models from odoo.exceptions import UserError try: import xlsxwriter import openpyxl except ImportError: xlsxwriter = None openpyxl = None _logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) class TicketBulkUpload(models.TransientModel): _name = 'ticket.bulk.upload' _description = 'Bulk Upload Tickets from Excel' upload_file = fields.Binary( string='Upload Excel File', help='Upload an Excel file with ticket data' ) upload_filename = fields.Char(string='Filename') template_file = fields.Binary( string='Template File', readonly=True, attachment=False ) template_filename = fields.Char( string='Template Filename', default='ticket_upload_template.xlsx', readonly=True ) import_summary = fields.Html( string='Import Summary', readonly=True ) def _resolve_user(self, user_input): """ Resolve user by abbreviation, full name, or email. Supports formats like: - EMAR (abbreviation - searches in name) - Eduardo Marquez (full name) - Ana Espinoza (full name) - user@example.com (email) """ if not user_input: return None user_input = str(user_input).strip() User = self.env['res.users'] # Try 1: Search by login/email if '@' in user_input: user = User.search([('login', '=ilike', user_input)], limit=1) if user: return user # Try 2: Search by abbreviation or name (case-insensitive) # Searches in name field for the input string user = User.search(['|', ('name', '=ilike', user_input), ('name', 'ilike', user_input)], limit=1) if user: return user return None def action_download_template(self): """Generate and download Excel template with proper columns and instructions.""" if not xlsxwriter: raise UserError(_('Python library xlsxwriter is required. Please install it.')) output = io.BytesIO() workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook(output, {'in_memory': True}) # Create main data sheet worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet('Tickets') # Define formats header_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#4472C4', 'font_color': 'white', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) required_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#E7E6E6', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) # Define columns (removed Activity Type and Tags) columns = [ ('Summary', 50, True, 'Brief description of the ticket (required)'), ('Description', 60, False, 'Detailed description in HTML or plain text'), ('Assigned To', 30, False, 'Name, abbreviation, or email (e.g., EMAR, Eduardo Marquez, user@example.com)'), ('Department', 25, False, 'Target department name'), ('Due Date', 15, False, 'Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2026-05-15)'), ('Priority', 10, False, 'P1, P2, or P3'), ('Group', 25, False, 'Activity group name'), ] # Write headers for col, (name, width, required, help_text) in enumerate(columns): worksheet.write(0, col, name, header_format if not required else required_format) worksheet.write_comment(0, col, help_text) worksheet.set_column(col, col, width) # Add sample data (updated without Activity Type and Tags) sample_data = [ [ 'Fix server connectivity issue', '

Server is not responding to ping requests. Need to investigate network configuration.

', 'EMAR', # Using abbreviation 'IT Department', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'P1', 'Infrastructure' ], [ 'Update employee handbook', 'Review and update the employee handbook with new policies', 'Ana Espinoza', # Using full name 'Human Resources', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=14)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'P2', 'HR' ], ] for row, data in enumerate(sample_data, start=1): for col, value in enumerate(data): worksheet.write(row, col, value) # Create instructions sheet instructions = workbook.add_worksheet('Instructions') instructions.set_column(0, 0, 80) instructions_text = [ ('Ticket Bulk Upload Instructions', header_format), ('', None), ('1. Fill in the "Tickets" sheet with your ticket data', None), ('2. Required field: Summary (highlighted in gray)', None), ('3. Optional fields will use defaults if left empty:', None), (' - Assigned To: Leave empty to assign to yourself', None), (' - Due Date: Leave empty to use default (today)', None), (' - Priority: P1, P2, or P3 (leave empty for no priority)', None), ('', None), ('4. Save the file and upload it using the bulk upload wizard', None), ('5. The system will create tickets and show you a summary', None), ('', None), ('Important Notes:', header_format), ('- Activity Type is always "To Do" (automatic)', None), ('- Assigned To supports:', None), (' * Abbreviations (e.g., EMAR for Eduardo Marquez)', None), (' * Full names (e.g., Eduardo Marquez, Ana Espinoza)', None), (' * Email addresses (e.g., user@example.com)', None), ('- Department names must exist in the system', None), ('- Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD', None), ('- Delete the sample rows before uploading', None), ('- Duplicate tickets (same summary + department + user) will be skipped', None), ] for row, (text, fmt) in enumerate(instructions_text): instructions.write(row, 0, text, fmt) workbook.close() output.seek(0) # Save to wizard template_data = base64.b64encode(output.read()) self.write({ 'template_file': template_data, 'template_filename': 'ticket_upload_template.xlsx' }) return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_url', 'url': f'/web/content?model={self._name}&id={self.id}&field=template_file&filename={self.template_filename}&download=true', 'target': 'self', } def _check_duplicate(self, summary, user_id, department_id): """Check if a similar ticket already exists to prevent duplicates.""" domain = [ ('summary', '=ilike', summary.strip()), ('user_id', '=', user_id), ] if department_id: domain.append(('target_department_id', '=', department_id)) # Check for existing open tickets (not done/cancelled) existing = self.env['mail.activity'].search(domain, limit=1) return bool(existing) def action_import_tickets(self): """Parse uploaded Excel file and create tickets.""" if not self.upload_file: raise UserError(_('Please upload an Excel file first.')) if not openpyxl: raise UserError(_('Python library openpyxl is required. Please install it.')) # Decode the file file_data = base64.b64decode(self.upload_file) workbook = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(file_data)) if 'Tickets' not in workbook.sheetnames: raise UserError(_('Excel file must contain a "Tickets" sheet.')) sheet = workbook['Tickets'] # Parse tickets created_count = 0 error_count = 0 duplicate_count = 0 errors = [] # Get column indices from header row header_row = list(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=1, values_only=True))[0] col_map = {name.strip(): idx for idx, name in enumerate(header_row) if name} # Get "To Do" activity type (default for all tickets) todo_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].search([('name', '=ilike', 'To Do')], limit=1) if not todo_type: # Create it if it doesn't exist todo_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].create({'name': 'To Do'}) # Process data rows (skip header) for row_num, row in enumerate(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True), start=2): if not any(row): # Skip empty rows continue try: # Extract values (updated column mapping) summary = row[col_map.get('Summary', 0)] if not summary: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Summary is required') error_count += 1 continue description = row[col_map.get('Description', 1)] or '' assigned_input = row[col_map.get('Assigned To', 2)] department_name = row[col_map.get('Department', 3)] due_date_str = row[col_map.get('Due Date', 4)] priority = row[col_map.get('Priority', 5)] group_name = row[col_map.get('Group', 6)] # Prepare ticket values vals = { 'summary': summary, 'note': description, 'activity_type_id': todo_type.id, # Always "To Do" } # Resolve assigned user (improved logic) user = None if assigned_input: user = self._resolve_user(assigned_input) if user: vals['user_id'] = user.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: User not found: {assigned_input}') error_count += 1 continue else: user = self.env.user vals['user_id'] = user.id # Resolve department department = None if department_name: department = self.env['hr.department'].search([('name', '=ilike', department_name)], limit=1) if department: vals['target_department_id'] = department.id vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('hr.department') vals['res_id'] = department.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Department not found: {department_name}') error_count += 1 continue else: # Default to a generic model if no department vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('res.users') vals['res_id'] = vals['user_id'] # Check for duplicates if self._check_duplicate(summary, vals['user_id'], vals.get('target_department_id')): errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Duplicate ticket skipped (same summary, user, and department)') duplicate_count += 1 continue # Parse due date if due_date_str: if isinstance(due_date_str, datetime): vals['date_deadline'] = due_date_str.date() else: try: vals['date_deadline'] = datetime.strptime(str(due_date_str), '%Y-%m-%d').date() except ValueError: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Invalid date format: {due_date_str}. Use YYYY-MM-DD') error_count += 1 continue else: vals['date_deadline'] = fields.Date.today() # Set priority if priority and str(priority).upper() in ['P1', 'P2', 'P3']: vals['priority'] = str(priority).lower() # Resolve group if group_name: group = self.env['acg.activity.group'].search([('name', '=ilike', group_name)], limit=1) if group: vals['group_id'] = group.id # Create the ticket self.env['mail.activity'].create(vals) created_count += 1 except Exception as e: _logger.exception(f'Error processing row {row_num}') errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: {str(e)}') error_count += 1 continue # Generate summary summary_html = f"""

Import Complete

Successfully created: {created_count} tickets

Duplicates skipped: {duplicate_count}

Errors: {error_count}

""" if errors: summary_html += '

Error Details:

    ' for error in errors[:50]: # Limit to first 50 errors summary_html += f'
  • {error}
    • ' if len(errors) > 50: summary_html += f'
  • ... and {len(errors) - 50} more errors
    • ' summary_html += '
' summary_html += '
' self.import_summary = summary_html return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 'res_model': self._name, 'res_id': self.id, 'view_mode': 'form', 'target': 'new', }

content.text:
## ✅ Bulk Upload Updated with Improvements!

I've updated the bulk upload feature with all your requested changes:

### **Changes Made:**

1. **✅ Removed Activity Type Column**
   - All tickets automatically use "To Do" activity type
   - Simplified the template

2. **✅ Removed Tags Column**
   - Tags removed from template and import logic

3. **✅ Smart User Assignment**
   - Now supports **3 formats** for "Assigned To":
     - **Abbreviations**: `EMAR`, `AE`, etc.
     - **Full Names**: `Eduardo Marquez`, `Ana Espinoza`
     - **Email**: `user@example.com`
   - Searches user's name field for matches
   - Falls back to current user if left empty

4. **✅ Duplicate Detection**
   - Automatically skips duplicate tickets
   - Checks: Same Summary + Same User + Same Department
   - Reports duplicates in import summary

### **Updated Template Columns:**

| Column | Required | Example Values |
|--------|----------|----------------|
| **Summary** | ✅ Yes | "Fix server connectivity issue" |
| **Description** | No | HTML or plain text |
| **Assigned To** | No | EMAR, Eduardo Marquez, or user@example.com |
| **Department** | No | IT Department, Human Resources |
| **Due Date** | No | 2026-05-15 |
| **Priority** | No | P1, P2, or P3 |
| **Group** | No | Infrastructure, HR |

### **How It Works:**

**User Resolution Logic:**
1. If contains `@` → searches by email
2. Otherwise → searches in user's name field (supports abbreviations like EMAR or full names like Eduardo Marquez)
3. Case-insensitive matching

**Duplicate Prevention:**
- Before creating each ticket, checks if one already exists with:
  - Same summary (case-insensitive)
  - Same assigned user
  - Same department
- If found, skips creation and reports as duplicate

### **Import Summary Now Shows:**
- ✅ Successfully created tickets
- ⚠️ Duplicates skipped
- ❌ Errors with row numbers

The module has been upgraded and the container restarted. Ready to use! 🎉

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from odoo import fields, models


class ResUsers(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'res.users'

    ticket_abbreviation = fields.Char(
        string='Ticket Abbreviation',
        help='Short code for bulk ticket upload (e.g., EMAR, AE, OMO)',
        index=True
    )

    _sql_constraints = [
        ('unique_ticket_abbreviation',
         'UNIQUE(ticket_abbreviation)',
         'Ticket abbreviation must be unique!')
    ]

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """Bulk ticket upload wizard using Excel templates.""" import base64 import io import json import logging from datetime import datetime, timedelta from odoo import _, api, fields, models from odoo.exceptions import UserError try: import xlsxwriter import openpyxl except ImportError: xlsxwriter = None openpyxl = None _logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) class TicketBulkUpload(models.TransientModel): _name = 'ticket.bulk.upload' _description = 'Bulk Upload Tickets from Excel' upload_file = fields.Binary( string='Upload Excel File', help='Upload an Excel file with ticket data' ) upload_filename = fields.Char(string='Filename') template_file = fields.Binary( string='Template File', readonly=True, attachment=False ) template_filename = fields.Char( string='Template Filename', default='ticket_upload_template.xlsx', readonly=True ) import_summary = fields.Html( string='Import Summary', readonly=True ) # Abbreviation mapping fields state = fields.Selection([ ('upload', 'Upload File'), ('map_abbreviations', 'Map Abbreviations'), ('import', 'Import Complete'), ], default='upload', string='State') unmapped_abbreviations = fields.Text( string='Unmapped Abbreviations', help='JSON data of abbreviations that need mapping' ) abbreviation_line_ids = fields.One2many( 'ticket.bulk.upload.abbreviation.line', 'wizard_id', string='Abbreviation Mappings' ) def _resolve_user(self, user_input): """ Resolve user by abbreviation or email. Returns user or None if not found. """ if not user_input: return None user_input = str(user_input).strip() User = self.env['res.users'] # Try 1: Exact abbreviation match user = User.search([('ticket_abbreviation', '=', user_input)], limit=1) if user: return user # Try 2: Search by login/email if '@' in user_input: user = User.search([('login', '=ilike', user_input)], limit=1) if user: return user return None def action_download_template(self): """Generate and download Excel template with proper columns and instructions.""" if not xlsxwriter: raise UserError(_('Python library xlsxwriter is required. Please install it.')) output = io.BytesIO() workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook(output, {'in_memory': True}) # Create main data sheet worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet('Tickets') # Define formats header_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#4472C4', 'font_color': 'white', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) required_format = workbook.add_format({ 'bold': True, 'bg_color': '#E7E6E6', 'border': 1, 'align': 'center', 'valign': 'vcenter', 'text_wrap': True }) # Define columns columns = [ ('Summary', 50, True, 'Brief description of the ticket (required)'), ('Description', 60, False, 'Detailed description in HTML or plain text'), ('Assigned To', 30, False, 'User abbreviation or email (e.g., EMAR, user@example.com)'), ('Department', 25, False, 'Target department name'), ('Due Date', 15, False, 'Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2026-05-15)'), ('Priority', 10, False, 'P1, P2, or P3'), ('Group', 25, False, 'Activity group name'), ] # Write headers for col, (name, width, required, help_text) in enumerate(columns): worksheet.write(0, col, name, header_format if not required else required_format) worksheet.write_comment(0, col, help_text) worksheet.set_column(col, col, width) # Add sample data sample_data = [ [ 'Fix server connectivity issue', '

Server is not responding to ping requests. Need to investigate network configuration.

', 'EMAR', # Using abbreviation 'IT Department', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'P1', 'Infrastructure' ], [ 'Update employee handbook', 'Review and update the employee handbook with new policies', 'AE', # Using abbreviation 'Human Resources', (datetime.now() + timedelta(days=14)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'P2', 'HR' ], ] for row, data in enumerate(sample_data, start=1): for col, value in enumerate(data): worksheet.write(row, col, value) # Create instructions sheet instructions = workbook.add_worksheet('Instructions') instructions.set_column(0, 0, 80) instructions_text = [ ('Ticket Bulk Upload Instructions', header_format), ('', None), ('1. Fill in the "Tickets" sheet with your ticket data', None), ('2. Required field: Summary (highlighted in gray)', None), ('3. Optional fields will use defaults if left empty:', None), (' - Assigned To: Leave empty to assign to yourself', None), (' - Due Date: Leave empty to use default (today)', None), (' - Priority: P1, P2, or P3 (leave empty for no priority)', None), ('', None), ('4. Save the file and upload it using the bulk upload wizard', None), ('5. If new abbreviations are found, you will be asked to map them', None), ('6. The system will create tickets and show you a summary', None), ('', None), ('Important Notes:', header_format), ('- Activity Type is always "To Do" (automatic)', None), ('- Assigned To supports:', None), (' * User abbreviations (e.g., EMAR, AE, OMO)', None), (' * Email addresses (e.g., user@example.com)', None), ('- New abbreviations will prompt for user selection', None), ('- Abbreviations are saved for future uploads', None), ('- Department names must exist in the system', None), ('- Date format must be YYYY-MM-DD', None), ('- Delete the sample rows before uploading', None), ('- Duplicate tickets (same summary + department + user) will be skipped', None), ] for row, (text, fmt) in enumerate(instructions_text): instructions.write(row, 0, text, fmt) workbook.close() output.seek(0) # Save to wizard template_data = base64.b64encode(output.read()) self.write({ 'template_file': template_data, 'template_filename': 'ticket_upload_template.xlsx' }) return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_url', 'url': f'/web/content?model={self._name}&id={self.id}&field=template_file&filename={self.template_filename}&download=true', 'target': 'self', } def _scan_file_for_abbreviations(self): """Scan uploaded Excel file and return list of unique 'Assigned To' values.""" if not self.upload_file or not openpyxl: return [] file_data = base64.b64decode(self.upload_file) workbook = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(file_data)) if 'Tickets' not in workbook.sheetnames: raise UserError(_('Excel file must contain a "Tickets" sheet.')) sheet = workbook['Tickets'] header_row = list(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=1, values_only=True))[0] col_map = {name.strip(): idx for idx, name in enumerate(header_row) if name} assigned_to_col = col_map.get('Assigned To', 2) assigned_values = set() for row in sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True): if not any(row): continue assigned_to = row[assigned_to_col] if assigned_to: assigned_values.add(str(assigned_to).strip()) return list(assigned_values) def action_validate_file(self): """Validate uploaded file and check for unmapped abbreviations.""" if not self.upload_file: raise UserError(_('Please upload an Excel file first.')) # Scan for all assigned_to values assigned_values = self._scan_file_for_abbreviations() # Check which ones don't have mappings unmapped = [] for value in assigned_values: if not value or '@' in value: # Skip empty or email continue user = self._resolve_user(value) if not user: unmapped.append(value) if unmapped: # Create abbreviation lines for mapping self.abbreviation_line_ids.unlink() for abbr in unmapped: self.env['ticket.bulk.upload.abbreviation.line'].create({ 'wizard_id': self.id, 'abbreviation': abbr, }) self.state = 'map_abbreviations' return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 'res_model': self._name, 'res_id': self.id, 'view_mode': 'form', 'target': 'new', } else: # No unmapped abbreviations, proceed directly to import return self.action_import_tickets() def action_save_abbreviations(self): """Save abbreviation mappings to user records.""" for line in self.abbreviation_line_ids: if not line.user_id: raise UserError(_('Please select a user for abbreviation: %s') % line.abbreviation) # Update user's abbreviation field line.user_id.ticket_abbreviation = line.abbreviation # Proceed to import return self.action_import_tickets() def _check_duplicate(self, summary, user_id, department_id): """Check if a similar ticket already exists to prevent duplicates.""" domain = [ ('summary', '=ilike', summary.strip()), ('user_id', '=', user_id), ] if department_id: domain.append(('target_department_id', '=', department_id)) existing = self.env['mail.activity'].search(domain, limit=1) return bool(existing) def action_import_tickets(self): """Parse uploaded Excel file and create tickets.""" if not self.upload_file: raise UserError(_('Please upload an Excel file first.')) if not openpyxl: raise UserError(_('Python library openpyxl is required. Please install it.')) # Decode the file file_data = base64.b64decode(self.upload_file) workbook = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(file_data)) if 'Tickets' not in workbook.sheetnames: raise UserError(_('Excel file must contain a "Tickets" sheet.')) sheet = workbook['Tickets'] # Parse tickets created_count = 0 error_count = 0 duplicate_count = 0 errors = [] # Get column indices from header row header_row = list(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=1, values_only=True))[0] col_map = {name.strip(): idx for idx, name in enumerate(header_row) if name} # Get "To Do" activity type (default for all tickets) todo_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].search([('name', '=ilike', 'To Do')], limit=1) if not todo_type: todo_type = self.env['mail.activity.type'].create({'name': 'To Do'}) # Process data rows (skip header) for row_num, row in enumerate(sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True), start=2): if not any(row): # Skip empty rows continue try: # Extract values summary = row[col_map.get('Summary', 0)] if not summary: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Summary is required') error_count += 1 continue description = row[col_map.get('Description', 1)] or '' assigned_input = row[col_map.get('Assigned To', 2)] department_name = row[col_map.get('Department', 3)] due_date_str = row[col_map.get('Due Date', 4)] priority = row[col_map.get('Priority', 5)] group_name = row[col_map.get('Group', 6)] # Prepare ticket values vals = { 'summary': summary, 'note': description, 'activity_type_id': todo_type.id, } # Resolve assigned user (exact abbreviation match or email) user = None if assigned_input: user = self._resolve_user(assigned_input) if user: vals['user_id'] = user.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: User abbreviation not found: {assigned_input}') error_count += 1 continue else: user = self.env.user vals['user_id'] = user.id # Resolve department department = None if department_name: department = self.env['hr.department'].search([('name', '=ilike', department_name)], limit=1) if department: vals['target_department_id'] = department.id vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('hr.department') vals['res_id'] = department.id else: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Department not found: {department_name}') error_count += 1 continue else: vals['res_model_id'] = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('res.users') vals['res_id'] = vals['user_id'] # Check for duplicates if self._check_duplicate(summary, vals['user_id'], vals.get('target_department_id')): errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Duplicate ticket skipped (same summary, user, and department)') duplicate_count += 1 continue # Parse due date if due_date_str: if isinstance(due_date_str, datetime): vals['date_deadline'] = due_date_str.date() else: try: vals['date_deadline'] = datetime.strptime(str(due_date_str), '%Y-%m-%d').date() except ValueError: errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: Invalid date format: {due_date_str}. Use YYYY-MM-DD') error_count += 1 continue else: vals['date_deadline'] = fields.Date.today() # Set priority if priority and str(priority).upper() in ['P1', 'P2', 'P3']: vals['priority'] = str(priority).lower() # Resolve group if group_name: group = self.env['acg.activity.group'].search([('name', '=ilike', group_name)], limit=1) if group: vals['group_id'] = group.id # Create the ticket self.env['mail.activity'].create(vals) created_count += 1 except Exception as e: _logger.exception(f'Error processing row {row_num}') errors.append(f'Row {row_num}: {str(e)}') error_count += 1 continue # Generate summary summary_html = f"""

Import Complete

Successfully created: {created_count} tickets

Duplicates skipped: {duplicate_count}

Errors: {error_count}

""" if errors: summary_html += '

Error Details:

    ' for error in errors[:50]: # Limit to first 50 errors summary_html += f'
  • {error}
    • ' if len(errors) > 50: summary_html += f'
  • ... and {len(errors) - 50} more errors
    • ' summary_html += '
' summary_html += '
' self.import_summary = summary_html self.state = 'import' return { 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 'res_model': self._name, 'res_id': self.id, 'view_mode': 'form', 'target': 'new', } class TicketBulkUploadAbbreviationLine(models.TransientModel): _name = 'ticket.bulk.upload.abbreviation.line' _description = 'Abbreviation to User Mapping Line' wizard_id = fields.Many2one('ticket.bulk.upload', required=True, ondelete='cascade') abbreviation = fields.Char(string='Abbreviation', required=True, readonly=True) user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Select User', required=True, domain=[('share', '=', False)])

<odoo>
    <!-- Add Ticket Abbreviation field to user form -->
    <record id="view_users_form_ticket_abbreviation" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">res.users.form.ticket.abbreviation</field>
        <field name="model">res.users</field>

                <field name="ticket_abbreviation"

                       help="Short code for bulk ticket upload"/>

        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

{
    "name": "Ticket System Module",
    "version": "18.0.1.1.7",
    "license": "AGPL-3",
    "author": "Prayas Thapa <itengineer@acglp.com>, Omar Morales <omorales@acglp.com>",
    "category": "Activity",
    "summary": "Uses existing activity functionality in Odoo as a ticket system",
    "description": "Departmental tickets on mail.activity: persistence, growth plans, Microsoft Graph mail, kanban patch.",
    "depends": ["base", "mail", "hr", "activity_modification", "document_library", "hr_employee_certifications", "hr_employee_growth"],
    "external_dependencies": {
        "python": ["requests", "xlsxwriter", "openpyxl"],
    },
    'assets': {
        'web.assets_backend': [
            'ticket_system/static/src/js/activity_kanban_patch.js',
        ],
    },
    "data": [
             "security/department_ticket_groups.xml",
             "security/ir.model.access.csv",
             "views/ticket_view.xml",
             "views/ticket_wizard_view.xml",
             "views/ticket_update_view.xml",
             "views/activity_plan_template.xml",
             "views/activity_bulk_edit_view.xml",
             "views/hr_department_anchor_ticket_columns.xml",
             "views/hr_department_project.xml",
             "views/hr_employee.xml",
